title: Tree Node
description: Tree Node renders a single hierarchical row with optional nested children, chevrons, selection indicators, custom content, quick actions, and drop targets.
intro: Use `ui-tree-node` when you need one expandable branch or want fine-grained control over recursive rows. For full collections of root nodes, use `ui-tree` instead.

featureSections.title: Leaf and parent rows
featureSections.description: A tree node can represent either a single leaf item or a parent row that exposes nested children. This is the smallest useful mental model for the component.

featureSections.title: Interaction behavior
featureSections.description: Chevron position, button-like rendering, and selection indicator placement change how the row feels. Use calmer behavior for file structures and stronger interaction for command-style rows.

featureSections.title: Common states
featureSections.description: Selected and disabled states should remain obvious even when the node is nested. Keep them consistent with the surrounding tree or navigation pattern.

featureSections.title: Custom content templates
featureSections.description: A custom row template is useful when nodes need metadata such as sync state, timestamps, or item details alongside the main label.

featureSections.title: Quick actions
featureSections.description: Quick actions fit management or explorer surfaces where users may rename, archive, or otherwise act on a row in place.

featureSections.title: Recursive nesting
featureSections.description: `ui-tree-node` can render nested children recursively. This is useful for one focused branch, embedded subtrees, or custom shells that do not need the full `ui-tree` container.

featureSections.title: Drop targets inside a branch
featureSections.description: Drop-zone support on `ui-tree-node` is useful when a single branch needs inline reordering or item moves without rendering a separate tree container.

accessibility.title: Tree-item semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-tree-node` exposes `role="treeitem"` and reflects hierarchical state through attributes such as `aria-expanded`, `aria-selected`, `aria-disabled`, and `aria-level` when applicable.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior follows the tree-item model.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Enter / Space | Activates the focused node. |
| ArrowRight | Expands a collapsed parent node. |
| ArrowLeft | Collapses an expanded parent node. |
| * | Expands sibling branches at the same level when supported by the current context. |

accessibility.title: Custom content and quick actions
accessibility.body: If you replace the default row content with a custom template, preserve a clear primary label. Quick actions and extra controls need their own accessible labels and should not make the main tree-item purpose ambiguous.

apiSections.title: TreeNodeComponent - ui-tree-node
apiSections.lead: Recursive standalone row component for hierarchical content. It can render one node with children, expose custom content and quick actions, and participate in selection, keyboard interaction, and drag-and-drop flows.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Corner treatment of the row container.
Renders the node with a more button-like interaction style.
Expands parent nodes when the row itself is clicked.
Marks the row as selected on click.
Enables an actions region in the row.
Template rendered in the quick-actions region.
Template that replaces the default icon and label content.
Shows child drop targets and accepts drops.

blocks.title: Outputs and node shape
rows: Emits when the row is activated.
Emits when a parent node expands or collapses.
Emits when the row selection changes.
Emit while dragging over or dropping before, after, or inside a child target.
Recursive node contract used by the component.

paragraphs: Use `ui-tree-node` directly for one recursive branch or embedded subtree patterns. For a full tree root collection, `ui-tree` usually gives a clearer API.
When you provide custom templates, keep the row compact and avoid burying the primary label under too much secondary metadata.
